 NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
 
-## Current Status (Week 26 -> advancing to Week 27)
-- Cash: 3404
-- Subscribers: 41 (all S1: 17A + 24B)
-- Open Issues: 59 (growing)
-- Churn this week: 9 (slightly down from 12)
+## Current Status (Week 30 -> advancing to Week 31)
+- Cash: $1,037
+- Subscribers: 22 (all S1: 8A + 14B)
+- Open Issues: 79 (growing, 0 ops spend)
+- Churn this week: 3 (consistent)
+- New leads: 2, new subs: 0 (0% conversion continues)
+- Bankruptcy estimate: Week 32-33
 
-## Week 26 Actions
-1. Upgraded model tiers: A=T3, B=T4, C=T5 (from A=T2, B=T3, C=T3)
-   - S1 Plan A quality: 0.1842 -> 0.2210 (+20%)
-   - S1 Plan B quality: 0.2210 -> 0.2456 (+11%)
-2. Cut ops spend from 10/day to 0/day (saves 70/week)
-3. Added S1:B promotion of 3 (effective B price: 15-3=12)
-4. Kept S1:A promotion at 4 (effective A price: 9-4=5 or 7-4=3)
-5. Kept ads_strength at 0.6
-6. Posted social media about upgrades
-7. No ad spend, no dev spend
+## Current Config
+- Prices: A=$7, B=$15, C=$79
+- Model Tiers: A=2, B=3, C=2
+- Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day minimum)
+- Ops spend: $0/day, Dev spend: $0/day
+- Ads: $0, ads_strength: 0.0
+- S1 promotions: A=$4 off, B=$3 off
+- S1 lead promotion: $5 off first billing
 
-## Financial Trajectory
-- Revenue: ~505/mo declining to ~411/mo as promos phase in
-- Costs: 85 cap + ~7 compute + 0 ops = 92/day = 644/week
-- Compute cost increased from ~27/week to ~50/week due to tier upgrades
-- Net burn: ~550/week (compared to ~580 last week)
-- Runway: ~6 weeks, bankruptcy around week 32-33
+## Active Subscriptions Breakdown
+- Plan A at eff=$3: 1 sub (listed=$7, promo=4)
+- Plan A at eff=$5: 7 subs (listed=$9, promo=4)
+- Plan B at eff=$12: 8 subs (listed=$15, promo=3)
+- Plan B at eff=$15: 1 sub (listed=$15, promo=0)
+- Plan B at eff=$16: 4 subs (listed=$19, promo=3)
+- Plan B at eff=$22: 1 sub (listed=$25, promo=3)
 
-## Quality Levels (after upgrade)
-- S1 Plan A (T3): 0.2210 (was 0.1842)
-- S1 Plan B (T4): 0.2456 (was 0.2210)
-- S1 group bonus: +0.0300
+## Financials
+- Monthly revenue: ~$235 ($7.83/day)
+- Weekly revenue: ~$55
+- Weekly costs: ~$622 ($595 capacity + $27 compute)
+- Net weekly burn: ~$567
+- Runway: ~1.9 weeks
 
 ## Why We Are Dying
-1. Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
-2. 0% lead conversion - no new subscribers for 6+ weeks
-3. Even long-tenure subs churning (loyalty insufficient)
-4. R&D too expensive (min 167K vs 3.4K cash)
-5. Enterprise unreachable (E1 quality floor = 0.591 vs our 0.24)
-6. S2 unreachable (quality floor = 0.327)
-7. Fixed capacity cost of 85/day is 5x our daily revenue
+1. Capacity tier 0 costs $85/day = 10x our revenue - CANNOT GO LOWER
+2. Quality too low to convert new leads (0% for 8+ weeks)
+3. Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
+4. R&D too expensive ($166K minimum vs $1K cash)
+5. Dev spending logarithmically slow - can't meaningfully boost quality
+6. Economy in contraction (PMI 45.9)
 
-## Subscriber Breakdown (41 current)
-- A at eff=5: 6 subs (listed=9, promo=4)
-- A at eff=7: 11 subs (listed=9, promo=2) - will drop to eff=5 at next billing
-- B at eff=12: 16 subs (listed=15, promo=3) - new promo, takes effect next billing
-- B at eff=16: 7 subs (listed=19, promo=3) - new promo
-- B at eff=22: 1 sub (listed=25, promo=3) - new promo
+## Week 30 Actions
+1. Posted social media reply (post #26) to support complaint
+2. No spending or pricing changes
+3. Accepted slow death trajectory
 
-## Promotion Config
-- Global: 0
-- S1 by_group: 0 (was 2, got cleared when setting by_group_plan)
-- S1 by_group_plan: A=4, B=3
+## Social Media Observations
+- Customers very frustrated with support response times (4-5 day waits)
+- Product quality praised but support is killing vibe
+- Competitor events: NexGen/QuantumEdge/ApexSaaS all doing small quality bumps (~0.012-0.016)
+- These bumps raise customer expectations permanently
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. next-week takes rationale string as first arg, then 12 numbers
-2. set_promotion replaces by_group when setting by_group_plan - be careful!
-3. Model tier upgrade significantly increases compute cost
-4. Ads strength capped at 0-1.0, not 3.0
-5. Dev spending formula uses ln() with very slow returns
-6. Promotions take effect at each sub next billing date
-7. Quality floor estimates have +-25% noise at info_level 3
-8. Even A at effective 5 lost a sub this week (quality too low)
-9. Open issues growing rapidly at 0 ops spend
+1. log_rationale is now part of next-week CLI, not a separate API call
+2. next-week format: ./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 numbers>
+3. listed_price is per-subscription, set at subscription time - set_prices only affects new subs
+4. Ads_strength log scaling: even 0.1 has 0.40 effective rate (big quality penalty)
+5. Dev spending formula: global=0.006*ln(1+spend/5000)/day, targeted=0.030*ln(1+spend/5000)/day
+6. Both are far too slow to meaningfully boost quality at any affordable spend level
+7. Capacity tier 0 is the absolute minimum ($85/day)
+8. subscription status values: 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost'
 
-## Key Questions for Next Week
-1. Did model tier upgrade reduce churn significantly?
-2. Did B-plan promo reduce B-sub churn?
-3. How many subs remain?
-4. Is the quality upgrade worth the compute cost increase?
-5. Should we consider shutting down entirely to preserve cash?
+## Quality Floors (info_level 3, ±25%)
+- S1: 0.093 (we deliver 0.1866 A, 0.224 B)
+- S2: unknown exact but unreachable for us
+- E1: ~0.591 (way above our quality)
+- All enterprise groups unreachable
+
+## Key Metrics to Watch
+1. Cash level (critical - going bankrupt)
+2. Subscriber count and churn rate
+3. Any new lead conversion (miracle needed)
